justification: All pass criteria met: MD5 identified as cryptographically broken and unsuitable for security tokens (Critical severity); HMAC-SHA256 explicitly recommended as replacement; timing attack via string equality identified as Critical; crypto.timingSafeEqual recommended with Buffer usage note; timestamp design flaw noted (caller must capture same millisecond, race condition); safe_to_deploy false.

observations.text: MD5 correctly identified as cryptographically broken with concrete attack scenario (hashcat GPU brute-force of secretKey)

Correctly distinguishes hash vs HMAC — explicitly states 'HMAC is the correct primitive for keyed message authentication' and warns that switching to SHA-256 without HMAC would inherit length-extension attacks
Timing attack identified with clear exploitation methodology and crypto.timingSafeEqual fix including length-check guard
Timestamp design flaw captured as Important issue — race condition between generator's Date.now() and caller's capture
Goes beyond rubric: identifies missing expiry enforcement, delimiter injection in payload construction, and length-extension attack risk
Production readiness section thoughtfully addresses migration complexity — dual-validation window, token re-issuance, backward compatibility
Suggests JWT as a comprehensive alternative that solves multiple issues at once

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: MD5 identified as cryptographically broken and unsuitable for security tokens (critical_findings[0]); HMAC-SHA256 recommended as replacement with explanation of why HMAC over raw hash (critical_findings[0].fix + important_issues[0]); timing attack via string equality identified (critical_findings[1]); crypto.timingSafeEqual recommended with Buffer.from usage (critical_findings[1].fix); timestamp storage design flaw noted as caller-supplied parameter issue (important_issues[1]); safe_to_deploy false.

observations.text: MD5 flagged as Critical with concrete attack vectors (hashcat, rainbow tables, collision attacks) — not just called 'weak'
Correctly identified this as a hash-not-HMAC problem, including length-extension attack vulnerability as a separate important issue
Timing attack identified with practical exploitation detail (byte-by-byte reconstruction via response time measurement)
crypto.timingSafeEqual recommended with the important detail about length-check guard
Timestamp design flaw identified both as missing expiration enforcement and as a non-self-contained token design
Bonus: identified input validation issue with colon delimiter collision — a subtle real-world attack vector
Production readiness section includes migration strategy (dual-validation grace period) — shows operational maturity
Suggested JWT as a standard alternative, which is the industry-standard fix for custom token schemes

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: MD5 identified as cryptographically broken and unsuitable for security tokens (Critical severity); HMAC-SHA256 explicitly recommended as replacement with correct usage guidance; timing attack via string equality identified as a separate Critical finding; crypto.timingSafeEqual recommended with proper Buffer usage; timestamp design flaw noted (caller must supply timestamp, token not self-contained); safe_to_deploy false.

observations.text: MD5 correctly identified as Critical with specific attack feasibility details (4B hashes/sec on commodity GPU)
Timing attack identified as separate Critical finding with byte-by-byte reconstruction attack description
crypto.timingSafeEqual recommended with correct implementation detail (length check + Buffer.from)
HMAC-SHA256 recommended with explicit warning against key concatenation (length-extension attacks)
Timestamp design flaw elevated to Critical with clear explanation that tokens are not self-contained
Excellent additional finding: length-extension attack vulnerability from H(message || key) construction
Production readiness section includes practical migration plan for breaking hash algorithm change
Input validation issue on partnerId separator collision is a strong bonus finding
